gaps.feature: Skill Authoring — create-from-scratch editor (J1): scaffold wizard (name+intent → teach-blocks → pick-sections), collapsible Guide rail, the 11 first-class typed block kinds via slash-menu + drag-handle canvas, transform-in-place loss-matrix, file-block upload with dual real safety-scan (Scan A import-vetting + Scan B per-upload) + type-aware visualizers, and Publish (v1, NATIVE, safety/vetting scan, version history panel)
gaps.source_ref: orvex-prompt-studio-poc/design-artifacts/C-UX-Scenarios/skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §1 J1, §3.1 S1–S7, §4 (11 block kinds + loss-matrix + byte-fidelity)
gaps.why_uncovered: orvex-studio-ui plan.json's Discover/Skill-Viewer/Builder epic (E4) ships only 3 stories: E4-S1 marketplace search, E4-S2 read-only Skill Viewer, E4-S3 the Base+Delta Builder — and E4-S3's own AC1 scopes itself explicitly to 'Given a fork...' (the Make-it-yours/Builder surface at /app/s/:skillId/make, marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §3.2.3a), never to create-from-scratch at /app/create. No ui FR-UI id, epic, or story titles the scaffold wizard, the Guide rail, the slash-menu/typed-block palette teaching flow, the transform loss-matrix, or the dual file-scan pipeline. The api project's 'Skill data model + deterministic byte-stable render' and 'Library, Collections & Builder edit surface' stories are backend-only (grep of ui/bodies/*.md for 'slash menu', 'Guide rail', 'loss-matrix', 'wizard' returns nothing). This is a first-class, PO-confirmed (2026-06-16, all 11 open questions resolved) authoring surface with its own route (/app/create, /app/create/:id) — not a restatement of the Builder fork-editor.
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED — E4-S3 Builder (E4-S3.md AC1) is scoped explicitly 'Given a fork...'; no ui story titles the create-from-scratch scaffold wizard/Guide rail/11-block slash-menu/loss-matrix/dual-scan. skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §1 J1/§3.1/§4 confirmed present.

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Skill authoring create-from-scratch editor — scaffold wizard, Guide rail, 11 typed block kinds, file dual-scan
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **signed-in creator (Sam)**, I want **a scaffold wizard that teaches the 11 typed block kinds and hands off into a Notion-style block canvas with a re-enterable Guide rail**, so that **I can author a real skill from nothing without staring at a blank page, and publish it as v1 with a real safety/vetting scan** (POC J1, `/app/create` → `/app/create/:id`).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestScaffoldWizardToPublishV1` — an integration test asserting: (a) Skip at the scaffold step deposits ≥1 empty Section (never a blank canvas), (b) the slash-menu + drag-handle canvas supports add/reorder/transform across all 11 kinds with the loss-matrix confirm on lossy transforms, (c) a file block runs both Scan A (import-vetting posture) and Scan B (per-upload) before rendering the visualizer, and (d) Publish mints v1 NATIVE with a version-history panel and a 'New' badge (never 'Used by 0'). *Final H1–H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given the scaffold wizard, When the user enters name+intent and either picks starter sections or taps Skip, Then Skip deposits at least 1 empty Section and the canvas is never blank. *Assertion: X-empty invariant holds.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §3.1 S1–S2]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given the block canvas, When the user opens the slash menu or the drag-handle context menu, Then all 11 first-class kinds (Text, Instruction, Example, Placeholder, Rule, Conditional, Task-list, Context, Verbatim, File, Free-form/Raw) are insertable with type-appropriate widgets and inline plain-English validation. *Assertion: 11/11 kinds render their typed widget.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §4.1]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given a kind-to-kind transform, When it is lossy (e.g. Example→Text), Then a confirm names exactly what is lost and the original block is kept disabled (reversible), never destroyed; blocked transforms (anything→Verbatim that would reflow bytes) are refused. *Assertion: loss-matrix enforced; no silent data loss.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §4.4]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given a file block upload, When it is created, Then both Scan A (import/catalogue vetting posture) and Scan B (real per-upload in-editor scan) run, and a Flagged result quarantines the file (not rendered, skill still saves) with an honest inline error + recoverable placeholder. *Assertion: dual-scan pipeline; never silently dropped.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §3.1 S4, §8 Q9]
- [ ] **AC5** — Given Publish (J1.7), When it completes, Then the skill mints v1 as NATIVE with social ON, provenance 'Created in Orvex', a 'New' badge (never 'Used by 0'), and a version-history panel (browse + diff + revert) is available. *Assertion: publish → v1 NATIVE + version history.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §3.1 S6–S7]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: TestScaffoldWizardToPublishV1 (AC1/AC5).
- [ ] GREEN: scaffold wizard (name+intent → teach-blocks → pick-sections) with Skip-deposits-empty-Section (AC1); slash-menu + drag-handle canvas for all 11 kinds + keyboard reorder equivalent (AC2); transform loss-matrix with disable-not-destroy (AC3); file block dual-scan + visualizer pipeline (AC4); Publish → v1 NATIVE + version-history panel (AC5).

## 🧠 Context

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep — orvex-prompt-studio-poc/design-artifacts/C-UX-Scenarios (UX scenario specs, source-of-truth for the studio rewrite). This is distinct from E4-S3 (the fork-only Make-it-yours/Builder at `/app/s/:skillId/make`) — E4-S3's own scope line is 'Given a fork, When editing/adding/disabling/moving blocks...'. Create-from-scratch (J1) mints a fresh NATIVE draft with no parent, and owns its own scaffold-wizard teaching layer that E4-S3 does not model. Reuses the frozen deterministic render engine (no re-implementation) and the Base/Delta primitive from E4-S3 where the data model overlaps (a NATIVE draft is still Base+Delta from birth). React-front editor (CS §6 — the deterministic weave/render is presentational). Seam: BFF skill-create + Delta persist. Sibling dependency: E4-S2 (viewer host), E4-S3 (shared Base/Delta primitives), E1-S5 (honest states).

## 🧪 Testing

`TestScaffoldWizardToPublishV1` (integration) + unit tests on the loss-matrix + keyboard-reorder equivalents. CS §5 mocking: BFF skill-create/scan fixtures; never mock own canvas/wizard rendering.

## 📏 Guidance

- CS `6aMAzsYeQb`: §6 shallow view (no client-side domain logic — BFF/api own it) · §11 honesty (no black-box scores, no fabricated proof, honest empty states) · §3 naming.
- SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: honesty lens (no fake data/social proof); reuse-don't-redesign lens (cite the reused surfaces, don't re-spec them).

## 🔗 References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (candidate FR — not yet assigned; nearest existing FR-UI13 covers only the fork/edit Builder) · POC `skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md` (PO-confirmed 2026-06-16, all 11 open questions resolved) · MA3/MB3 canon.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] **Blocked by:** ENG-2109 (Definition Pack — the contract TAG is the dispatch gate).
- [ ] **Related:** E4-S3 (Builder) for the shared Base/Delta primitive; intra-epic order after E4-S3.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.feature: Skill Feedback overlay (J3 Comment + J4 Suggest): block-level hover+focus action strip on the read/typed-block skill view, sign-in-first composer anchored at {skillId,version,blockId}, per-type diff widgets for Suggest, Comment→Suggest bridge, and the unclaimed-skill redirect panel (Claim/Fork, not a wall)
gaps.why_uncovered: No ui epic/story mentions a block-level comment or suggest affordance. grep of ui/bodies/*.md for 'suggest', 'comment', 'github oauth' as feature keywords (excluding the CLAIM→PLAN→PROGRESS protocol footer, which is unrelated) returns nothing for E4 (Discover/Viewer/Builder) or any other epic. E4-S2 (Skill Viewer) explicitly scopes its injection slots as 'slot content owned by its feature's surface' but no surface claims the Comment/Suggest slot. This is a PO-confirmed (2026-06-16, all 11 open questions resolved), fully-live feedback loop distinct from the Improve-with-AI panel (which patches the user's own Delta, not a social suggestion to another owner).
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED — E4-S2 Skill Viewer is read-only typed-item render with unclaimed injection slots; no ui story owns block-level Comment/Suggest. skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §3.2 J3/J4 confirmed present; not a re-spec of E4-S3.

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Skill feedback overlay — block-level Comment + Suggest (sign-in-first, per-type diff)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **signed-in contributor (Lena or Sam)**, I want **a hover-and-focus action strip on any block of a claimed/native skill offering Comment (discussion, no change) and Suggest a change (per-type diff, batchable)**, so that **I can flag or fix a specific block without editing the skill directly, and never hit a dead end on an unclaimed skill** (POC J3/J4).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestBlockFeedbackCommentAndSuggest` — an integration test asserting: (a) the action strip is keyboard-focusable (not hover-only) on every block of a CLAIMED/NATIVE skill, (b) tapping Comment or Suggest on an unauthenticated session prompts Clerk sign-in before the composer opens, (c) a Suggest composer renders the correct per-type diff widget for the target block's kind, and (d) an UNCLAIMED skill renders the claim/fork redirect panel instead of the action strip. *Final H1–H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a block on a CLAIMED/NATIVE skill, When hovered OR keyboard-focused, Then a 💬 Comment / ✎ Suggest / ⋯ action strip renders (never hover-only). *Assertion: strip reachable by keyboard.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §3.2, P1-1]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given an unauthenticated user, When Comment or Suggest is tapped, Then Clerk sign-in opens first and the composer only opens after sign-in succeeds (FR-27 sign-in-first, no anon-draft mechanism). *Assertion: composer never opens pre-auth.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §1 J3.2/J4.1, §8 Q2]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given Suggest is opened on a block, When the composer renders, Then it uses the same per-type widget the owner's editor uses, pre-filled, rendered as a per-type diff (Text→word-level redline, Rule→polarity-change, Example→paired in/out, Placeholder→field table, Conditional→branch-logic, Task-list→row-level +/−/↕, Verbatim→byte-level with alteration warning, File→scan+visualizer diff), and is batchable across multiple blocks with a required one-line 'Why this change?'. *Assertion: correct diff widget per kind; batchable submit.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §4.3]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given a Comment, When posted, Then it never mutates the skill, is tagged with the author's reputation chip, and offers a one-tap 'Turn into a suggestion' bridge pre-anchored to the same block (linked; a reject on the derived suggestion returns the comment thread to Open). *Assertion: comment→suggestion bridge one-tap, linked.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §1 J3.4]
- [ ] **AC5** — Given an UNCLAIMED skill, When a viewer reaches where the action strip would render, Then an honest redirect panel shows instead ('Feedback opens once someone owns this skill' + Claim via GitHub OAuth or Fork) — never a silent absence or a wall. *Assertion: unclaimed redirect renders, not a dead-end.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §3.2 'Unclaimed redirect']

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: TestBlockFeedbackCommentAndSuggest (AC1/AC2/AC5).
- [ ] GREEN: keyboard-reachable action strip (AC1); sign-in-first gate (AC2); per-type diff composer + batching (AC3); comment posting + reputation chip + bridge (AC4); unclaimed redirect panel (AC5).

## 🧠 Context

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep — orvex-prompt-studio-poc/design-artifacts/C-UX-Scenarios (UX scenario specs, source-of-truth for the studio rewrite). The Improve-with-AI panel (E5-S1, FR-UI14) is a DIFFERENT feature — it patches the signed-in user's OWN Delta via a single-shot AI call, never a social suggestion visible to the skill owner. This story is the human-authored, owner-reviewed suggestion/comment loop that feeds the Review inbox (see companion gap G3). Reuses the deterministic per-type widget components from the Builder (E4-S3) for rendering, but the diff/composer logic and the Clerk sign-in gate are net-new. React-front overlay (CS §6). Seam: BFF comment/suggest persist + Clerk auth. Sibling dependency: E4-S2 (viewer host for the action-strip mount point), E4-S3 (typed-widget reuse).

## 🧪 Testing

`TestBlockFeedbackCommentAndSuggest` (integration) + unit tests on the per-type diff widget selection. CS §5 mocking: BFF comment/suggest fixtures; never mock own diff-widget rendering.

## 📏 Guidance

- CS `6aMAzsYeQb`: §6 shallow view (no client-side domain logic — BFF/api own it) · §11 honesty (no black-box scores, no fabricated proof, honest empty states) · §3 naming.
- SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: honesty lens (no fake data/social proof); reuse-don't-redesign lens (cite the reused surfaces, don't re-spec them).

## 🔗 References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (candidate FR — not yet assigned) · POC `skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md` §1 J3/J4, §3.2, §4.3 (PO-confirmed 2026-06-16) · FR-27 (sign-in-first, unchanged).

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] **Blocked by:** ENG-2109 (Definition Pack).
- [ ] **Related:** E4-S2 (Skill Viewer, mounts the action-strip injection slot); companion gap G3 (Review inbox) is the decision surface this feeds.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.feature: Review inbox (J5) unified queue at /review — per-block Accept/Reject + Accept-all/Reject-all with partial-accept, post-merge deterministic preview, conflict/needs-rebase handling, bulk-accept, and the contributor reputation ladder (New→Contributor→Reliable→Steward) rendered as a shared, itemized, decomposable chip
gaps.why_uncovered: No ui story exists for a Review inbox surface: grep of ui/bodies/*.md for 'reputation' returns only E2-S1 (the account-footer's static claim/reputation-chip DISPLAY, not the earning/ladder system) and E3-S3 (which explicitly asserts Memory carries NO reputation chrome — the opposite of this feature). '/review' as a route, the unified queue, per-block diff-stack decisioning, and the New→Contributor→Reliable→Steward ladder appear nowhere in the 30 ui story titles or FR-UI1..30. secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md independently corroborates this is a real, routed, dedicated surface (route table lists '#6 Review /review (badge)' as a sidebar destination, distinct from the inbox-drawer peek which explicitly has no Accept/Reject and only deep-links into /review for the decision).
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED — reputation appears only as E2-S1's static display chip and E3-S3's explicit no-chip-on-Memory rule; no /review queue/accept-reject story exists anywhere in ui. skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §3.3 J5 confirmed present.

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Review inbox (J5) — unified queue, per-block accept/reject, bulk-accept, contributor reputation ladder
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **signed-in skill owner (Sam)**, I want **one unified inbox at `/review` across all my skills, tagging each item Comment vs Suggestion with the contributor's reputation chip, supporting per-block accept/reject with partial-accept and bulk-accept for non-conflicting suggestions**, so that **I can triage feedback fast even at hundreds of items and never lose an unsaved Delta to an accidental auto-merge** (POC J5).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestReviewInboxPartialAcceptMergesViaJ25` — an integration test asserting: (a) the inbox renders a unified queue sorted by reputation with honest empty states (counts '—' while loading, never '0'), (b) a Suggestion opens as a per-type diff stack with independent per-block Accept/Reject plus a global Accept-all/Reject-all, (c) a partial-accept mints a new version through the exact same publish path a manual edit uses (never a second pipeline), and (d) a conflicting sibling suggestion is marked Needs-rebase (never auto-merged) with its diff recomputed against the new Base. *Final H1–H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given the inbox at `/review`, When it loads, Then it shows one unified queue across all the user's skills, each row tagged Comment vs Suggestion with a skill→section→block label and the contributor's reputation chip, default-sorted by highest reputation; counts render `—` while loading, never `0`. *Assertion: unified queue + honest loading state.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §3.3 A1]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a Suggestion item, When opened, Then it renders a per-type diff stack (one collapsible diff per touched block) with independent per-block Accept/Reject plus a global Accept-all/Reject-all (partial-accept supported; status derives to accepted/partially-accepted/rejected). *Assertion: partial-accept renders and is selectable.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §3.3 A2, §8 Q3]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given an Accept decision, When it commits, Then accepted blocks merge into the owner's Delta and mint a new version via the identical publish/re-vet path a manual edit uses (never a second pipeline), notify the contributor, and increment their reputation per accepted block. *Assertion: accept → same-path version mint; reputation increments.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §1 J5.4]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given two suggestions targeting the same block, When one is accepted, Then the other is marked Needs-rebase and its diff is recomputed against the new Base — never auto-merged. *Assertion: conflicting sibling → Needs-rebase, never silent merge.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §1 J5.4, §3.3 A2]
- [ ] **AC5** — Given a contributor's accepted-suggestion history, When their reputation chip is expanded (in the inbox, the Feedback overlay, or marketplace owner-verification — one shared component), Then it shows an itemized, decomposable New→Contributor→Reliable→Steward tier with real thresholds and 'X to next level' progress — never a black-box composite score, never on any Memory surface. *Assertion: reputation chip itemizes; never mounts on Memory.* [Source: POC skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md §5]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: TestReviewInboxPartialAcceptMergesViaJ25 (AC2/AC3/AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: unified queue + honest states (AC1); per-type diff stack + partial-accept UI (AC2); accept → same-path version mint + notify + reputation increment (AC3); conflict/needs-rebase detection (AC4); shared reputation chip component with itemized ladder (AC5).

## 🧠 Context

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep — orvex-prompt-studio-poc/design-artifacts/C-UX-Scenarios (UX scenario specs, source-of-truth for the studio rewrite). The Inbox Drawer (secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md §5.2, likely bundled with E2-S1's shell chrome) is explicitly a PEEK ONLY with no Accept/Reject — 'ALL decisions happen in /review.' This story is that decision surface; it must not be conflated with the drawer. Reuses the per-type diff widgets from the Feedback overlay (companion gap G2) for rendering. React-front review surface (CS §6). Seam: BFF suggestion/comment decision + version-mint. Sibling dependency: companion gap G2 (produces the items this inbox decides), E4-S3 (shared publish/version-mint path).

## 🧪 Testing

`TestReviewInboxPartialAcceptMergesViaJ25` (integration) + unit tests on conflict detection and the reputation-ladder itemization. CS §5 mocking: BFF review/reputation fixtures; never mock own diff-stack rendering.

## 📏 Guidance

- CS `6aMAzsYeQb`: §6 shallow view (no client-side domain logic — BFF/api own it) · §11 honesty (no black-box scores, no fabricated proof, honest empty states) · §3 naming.
- SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: honesty lens (no fake data/social proof); reuse-don't-redesign lens (cite the reused surfaces, don't re-spec them).

## 🔗 References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (candidate FR — not yet assigned) · POC `skill-authoring-scenario-and-spec.md` §1 J5, §3.3, §5 (PO-confirmed 2026-06-16, Q6/Q10 build-the-full-ladder decision).

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] **Blocked by:** ENG-2109 (Definition Pack).
- [ ] **Depends on:** companion gap G2 (Feedback overlay — produces the queued items).

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.feature: Skill Detail ownership/trust/social layer: GitHub-OAuth Claim flow (happy path + 4 distinct edge states), the 'Why Trusted?' decomposition panel, live Reviews posting (worked/partially/didn't verbal-distribution, use-gated), the Community zone container (live use-gated Reviews + the per-write fail-soft unclaimed-claim-panel swap — the block-level Comment/Suggest widgets themselves are companion gap G2's scope, reused not re-built here), and the full action matrix by ownership state (Anonymous/Claimed/Unclaimed/Fork)
gaps.source_ref: orvex-prompt-studio-poc/design-artifacts/C-UX-Scenarios/marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §3.2 Zones 1/4/5, §4 (ownership state machine + claim flow §4.4), §5 (trust signal set + 'Why Trusted?')
gaps.why_uncovered: orvex-studio-ui's E4-S2 (Skill Viewer) covers only the read-only typed-item render + per-block provenance + injection slots + raw fallback (FR-UI12) — it does not mention Claim, Trusted, Reviews, or Comments. grep of ui/bodies/*.md for 'github oauth' returns zero hits anywhere in the ui project; 'why trusted'/'trust panel'/'trusted badge' return zero hits; 'reputation' returns only the account-footer display chip (E2-S1) and Memory's explicit no-chip rule (E3-S3), neither of which is the marketplace claim/trust surface. This is Locked Decisions #3/#6/#7 (PO 2026-06-16/17) — a first-class, deeply-specified, fully-live requirement (Reviews explicitly reversed from demo-disabled to fully-live posting on 2026-06-17), not incidental chrome.
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED — E4-S2 has no Claim/Trust/Reviews; api's E2-S3 (reputation ledger) is backend-only, no GitHub-OAuth-claim story anywhere. marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §3.2/§4/§5 confirmed present. Tightened wording so the Community-zone AC (Reviews + unclaimed-panel swap only) doesn't re-litigate G2's Comment/Suggest widgets.

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Skill Detail — ownership/trust/social layer (GitHub Claim, Why-Trusted panel, live Reviews, Community zone)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **visitor deciding whether to trust a skill (Priya) or an owner managing it (Sam)**, I want **the Claim action (GitHub OAuth), a 'Why Trusted?' decomposition panel, live worked/partially/didn't reviews, and a Comments+Suggestions Community zone gated by ownership state**, so that **I can always answer 'why is this trusted' and the right actions are available for my relationship to the skill** (marketplace §3.2 Zones 1/4/5, §4, §5).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestSkillDetailOwnershipActionsAndTrustPanel` — an integration test asserting: (a) the action set (Copy/Save/Fork/Claim/Comment/Suggest/Review/Edit) renders exactly per the 5-column ownership-state matrix (Anonymous/Claimed-not-yours/Claimed-yours/Unclaimed/Fork-yours), (b) tapping 'Why Trusted?' opens a panel listing every criterion's actual status (never a composite score), (c) the Claim button drives GitHub OAuth via Clerk and renders all 4 distinct edge messages (cancelled/failed/unreachable/already-claimed) inline, and (d) a Review post is use-gated (rejected without a prior real use/copy event) and renders as a text-labelled non-color distribution. *Final H1–H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a skill in each of the 5 ownership/auth states, When the action bar renders, Then it shows exactly the actions the matrix permits (e.g. Claim only on Unclaimed + signed-in; Comment/Suggest/Review require sign-in and a CLAIMED/NATIVE owner state; Edit only on the owner's own). *Assertion: 5-state action matrix enforced.* [Source: POC marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §3.2.3]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given the Trust & freshness panel (Zone 4), When 'Why Trusted?' is tapped, Then a panel lists every named criterion (Safety-scan, Claimed/Verified-owner, Author credentials, Usage, Reviews, Lineage, Model freshness, Editorial) with THIS skill's actual status text — never a single composite number. *Assertion: decomposed criteria render, no composite score.* [Source: POC marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §5]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given Claim is tapped on an Unclaimed skill, When the GitHub OAuth round-trip runs via Clerk, Then it renders the happy-path confirm ('…its N saves stay with the skill') on match, and 4 DISTINCT inline messages for OAuth-cancel, OAuth-fail, GitHub-unreachable, and already-claimed-by-another (never a generic error, never a half-claimed state — claim is transactional). *Assertion: 4 distinct claim-edge messages; no half-claim.* [Source: POC marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §3.2.2, §4.4]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given a CLAIMED/NATIVE skill's Community zone (Zone 5), When a signed-in user posts a Review, Then it is rejected unless a real per-user use/copy event exists (use-gated), stores worked/partially/didn't as a raw text-percentage distribution (non-color pattern, no gameable star average), and is one-per-user latest-wins. On an UNCLAIMED skill the zone is replaced (not empty) by the claim-explainer panel. *Assertion: use-gate enforced; distribution text+pattern; unclaimed shows claim panel, not an empty zone.* [Source: POC marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §3.2.1 Zone 5, §5 signal 5]
- [ ] **AC5** — Given each live write in this surface (Claim/Comment/Suggest/Review), When it fails online, Then it fails-soft within its own control (inline error + retry) and never disables or blanks any other zone or action on the page — there is no global read-only degrade. *Assertion: per-write fail-soft; no global degrade.* [Source: POC marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §0-E, Locked Decision #2]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: TestSkillDetailOwnershipActionsAndTrustPanel (AC1/AC3/AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: ownership-state action matrix (AC1); Why-Trusted decomposition panel (AC2); Claim GitHub-OAuth flow with 4 edge states (AC3); Community zone with use-gated Reviews + unclaimed claim-panel swap (AC4); per-write fail-soft wiring (AC5).

## 🧠 Context

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep — orvex-prompt-studio-poc/design-artifacts/C-UX-Scenarios (UX scenario specs, source-of-truth for the studio rewrite). Layers on top of E4-S2's read-only typed-item render (which owns 'every declared injection slot mounts empty/clean' — this story fills the ownership/trust/social slot). Reuses the shared reputation chip component from companion gap G3 (Review inbox) for Community-zone attribution, and reuses companion gap G2's (Feedback overlay) Comment/Suggest widgets for the zone's discussion content — this story owns only the zone container, the Reviews sub-feature, and the unclaimed-panel swap, not the Comment/Suggest composer itself. React-front surface (CS §6 — the domain state machine and reputation ledger are owned by the api project's 'Marketplace, social & append-only reputation ledger' backend story). Seam: BFF claim/review/comment endpoints + Clerk GitHub OAuth. Sibling dependency: E4-S2 (viewer host), companion gap G3 (shared reputation chip).

## 🧪 Testing

`TestSkillDetailOwnershipActionsAndTrustPanel` (integration) + unit tests on the 5-state action matrix and the 4 claim-edge messages. CS §5 mocking: BFF claim/review/comment fixtures; never mock own action-matrix rendering.

## 📏 Guidance

- CS `6aMAzsYeQb`: §6 shallow view (no client-side domain logic — BFF/api own it) · §11 honesty (no black-box scores, no fabricated proof, honest empty states) · §3 naming.
- SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: honesty lens (no fake data/social proof); reuse-don't-redesign lens (cite the reused surfaces, don't re-spec them).

## 🔗 References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (candidate FR — not yet assigned; nearest existing FR-UI12 covers only the read-only viewer) · POC `marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md` §3.2, §4, §5 (Locked Decisions #3/#6/#7, PO refinement 2026-06-17 reversing Reviews to fully-live).

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] **Blocked by:** ENG-2109 (Definition Pack).
- [ ] **Related:** E4-S2 (Skill Viewer, hosts the injection slot); companion gap G3 (shared reputation chip); backend 'Marketplace, social & append-only reputation ledger' story in orvex-studio-api.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.why_uncovered: E4-S1's ('Marketplace home + search-as-you-type + facets + anon wall-free variant', FR-UI11) 5 acceptance criteria cover skeleton-to-card first paint, debounced search, facet composition, no-result guidance, and the anon wall-free variant — none mention clustering, collapse, '+N more', or query-aware head election. grep of ui/bodies/*.md for 'cluster'/'collapse' returns hits only in E3-S2, E2-S1, E6-S4 — none of which are the Discover epic (E4); those are unrelated uses of the word (form-canvas collapse, sidebar collapse, curation-queue grouping). This is a PO-locked (2026-06-16/17, Locked Decision + §0-D) discovery-presentation feature with real anti-land-grab UI rules distinct from plain search/facet rendering.
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED — E4-S1 covers only facets/search-as-you-type, no clustering; §0-D/§0-E confirmed present verbatim in marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md. Fixed a false Context claim that the BFF 'already returns cluster_id/is_cluster_head' — grep of every breakdown/*/bodies/*.md shows cluster_id/is_cluster_head/lineage_root_id is unticketed anywhere (including api), so this UI story has an undeclared upstream backend dependency; noted in Dependencies rather than assumed as already-built.

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Discover — cluster/dedupe collapse UI (+N more, query-aware head, lineage-root anchoring)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **visitor browsing Discover (Priya)**, I want **near-duplicate/fork clusters collapsed under one representative card with a '+N more' expander**, so that **I see one clean row per topic instead of a wall of near-identical cards, while the original author is never silently buried by a fork** (marketplace §2, §3.1.3).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestClusterCollapseQueryAwareHeadNeverBuriesRoot` — an integration test asserting: (a) a materialized cluster renders as one card + a '+N more ›' footer that expands inline to independently-countable members, (b) with a query present the displayed head is the highest-blended-score member for THAT query (not always the lineage root), (c) when a fork legitimately out-earns the root the header reads 'top pick — a fork of [Original by octocat]' and the root is still named (never silently demoted), and (d) engaging any trust/freshness/claimed facet or an explicit sort flips the view to flat (a) — Sam's power-user signal. *Final H1–H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a materialized cluster (lineage or semantic), When it renders in results, Then it shows one head card with a '+N more ›' (or '+N similar ›' for semantic) footer that expands INLINE to a compact stacked list of members, each with its own independent (often low/zero) counts. *Assertion: collapse renders; expand is inline, no route change.* [Source: POC marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §3.1.3]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a search query is present, When head election runs, Then the displayed representative is the highest blended-score member FOR THAT QUERY (DISTINCT ON cluster_id ORDER BY blended DESC) — not simply the lineage root — while Browse (no query) collapses on the lineage root. *Assertion: query-aware head differs correctly from the no-query browse head.* [Source: POC marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §0-D, §3.1.3]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given a fork legitimately out-earning its parent by a large sustained verification-weighted margin, When it becomes the displayed head, Then the header reads 'top pick — a fork of [Original by octocat]' and the original is never silently demoted from view. *Assertion: co-representative naming always cites the root.* [Source: POC marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §0-D]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given the collapsed view is active, When the user engages a trust/freshness/claimed facet or an explicit sort, Then the view flips to flat (a) automatically (never requiring a manual toggle for that specific case); the explicit 'Group similar (on)/Show all (off)' toggle is also available directly. *Assertion: power-facet engagement auto-flips to flat; manual toggle also works.* [Source: POC marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §2 'Toggle behavior']
- [ ] **AC5** — Given a freshly-forked skill, When it is created, Then it collapses under its parent cluster immediately (cluster_id/lineage_root_id set synchronously at fork INSERT — no stray top-level-row race on demo day). *Assertion: new fork appears grouped, not as a stray row.* [Source: POC marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md §3.1.6]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: TestClusterCollapseQueryAwareHeadNeverBuriesRoot (AC2/AC3/AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: cluster head card + inline '+N more' expander (AC1); query-aware head selection wiring (AC2); lineage-root-anchored naming copy (AC3); facet-engagement auto-flip-to-flat + explicit toggle (AC4); fork-at-insert grouping (AC5).

## 🧠 Context

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep — orvex-prompt-studio-poc/design-artifacts/C-UX-Scenarios (UX scenario specs, source-of-truth for the studio rewrite). This sits inside E4-S1's Discover surface (reads the materialized cluster_id/is_cluster_head columns the api's marketplace backend story computes — the UI does no clustering itself, only presentation). Distinct from facet/search rendering, which E4-S1 already covers. React-front presentation layer (CS §6 — no client-side clustering logic). Seam: BFF /api/skills read, PENDING an upstream backend gap — grep of every breakdown/*/bodies/*.md (incl. api's E2-S3 reputation-ledger story) finds no story that computes/exposes cluster_id/is_cluster_head/lineage_root_id; this UI story cannot be verified end-to-end until that backend gap is also filed and its API contract lands. Sibling dependency: E4-S1 (hosts the results region this collapse renders inside).

## 🧪 Testing

`TestClusterCollapseQueryAwareHeadNeverBuriesRoot` (integration) + unit tests on head-election selection logic. CS §5 mocking: BFF cluster-bearing skill-list fixtures; never mock own expand/collapse rendering.

## 📏 Guidance

- CS `6aMAzsYeQb`: §6 shallow view (no client-side domain logic — BFF/api own it) · §11 honesty (no black-box scores, no fabricated proof, honest empty states) · §3 naming.
- SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: honesty lens (no fake data/social proof); reuse-don't-redesign lens (cite the reused surfaces, don't re-spec them).

## 🔗 References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (candidate FR — not yet assigned; nearest existing FR-UI11 covers only search/facets, not clustering) · POC `marketplace-scenario-and-spec.md` §0-D, §2, §3.1.3 (Locked Decisions #2/#12, PO refinement 2026-06-17 §15 tie-break rule).

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] **Blocked by:** ENG-2109 (Definition Pack).
- [ ] **Intra-epic order:** after E4-S1 (extends its results region).
- [ ] **Needs companion backend gap:** no api/knowledge story computes cluster_id/is_cluster_head/lineage_root_id — file alongside api's E2-S3 (Marketplace, social & append-only reputation ledger) before this story can be end-to-end verified.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.feature: Account & Settings full IA at /account: Connections panel (GitHub connect/reconnect/disconnect status + claimed-skills list + reputation chip + Your Wiki deep-link card), Notifications/digest matrix (per-event × per-channel toggles, honest LIVE-vs-COMING), Danger zone (sign out + typed-confirmation account delete with the SC-2b unclaim-not-delete consequence copy), and the Appearance panel (light/dark/system theme radiogroup)
gaps.source_ref: orvex-prompt-studio-poc/design-artifacts/C-UX-Scenarios/secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md §3 (PANELS 1-6, esp. §3.3 Connections, §3.5 Notifications, §3.6 Appearance, §3.7 Danger zone)
gaps.why_uncovered: The only ui-side mention of account settings is E2-S1's shell-chrome GREEN task line ('account footer (Clerk <UserProfile/>, claim/reputation chip, sensitive-data controls, theme toggle, sign out)') — a one-line footer-MENU summary, not the 6-panel deep-linkable /account IA (§3.1 route table: /account/{connections,privacy,notifications,appearance,danger}). E3-S4 ('Sensitive-field consent UX + Enrichment Policy setting + hard-delete', FR-UI8) covers the Privacy panel's memory-specific behavior, but nothing in the 30 ui stories covers the Connections panel's GitHub reconnect/disconnect states, the Notifications event×channel matrix (a named notification_prefs store), the Danger-zone typed-confirmation account-delete flow with its ratified SC-2b consequence copy, or a dedicated Appearance settings screen (as opposed to the underlying theme-token infra in E1-S3). grep of ui/bodies/*.md confirms no 'notification_prefs', 'danger zone', or 'account delete' story exists.
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED — only E2-S1's one-line account-footer summary exists; no story covers the 6-panel /account IA. secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md §3 Panels 2/4/5/6 confirmed present verbatim (incl. 'Claim runs on Skill Detail, cited' — consistent with G4, not a duplicate).

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Account & Settings — Connections, Notifications, Danger zone, Appearance panels
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **signed-in user**, I want **a sectioned, deep-linkable Account IA (Connections, Notifications, Appearance, Danger zone) reached from the sidebar footer menu**, so that **I can manage my GitHub claim identity, notification channels, theme, and account deletion in one honest, autosaving settings surface** (secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md §3).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestAccountSettingsPanelsAutosaveAndDangerGates` — an integration test asserting: (a) each of the 4 net-new panels (Connections/Notifications/Appearance/Danger) deep-links via its own /account/{section} route, (b) every non-destructive control autosaves per-control with an inline 'Saved' pill (no global Save button), (c) the Notifications matrix renders each event×channel cell as LIVE or an honest 'coming' badge — never a fake working toggle, and (d) account delete requires typed-confirmation plus a fresh-session re-auth gate and renders the ratified unclaim-not-delete consequence copy for published-and-used skills. *Final H1–H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given the Account IA, When a user deep-links to /account/connections, /account/notifications, or /account/appearance, Then each renders its own section content within the shared 2-column settings shell (rail + content pane on desktop, full-screen sub-view on mobile). *Assertion: 3 net-new routes render distinct content.* [Source: POC secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md §3.1]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given the Connections panel, When rendered, Then it shows the GitHub connection status as one of Connected/Needs-reconnect/Not-connected (text+icon), a claimed-skills list with real 'Used by N' or 'New' (never 'Used by 0'), the reputation chip on claimed rows, and a compact Your-Wiki deep-link card — with 3 distinct OAuth-failure inline messages (cancelled/unreachable/failed), never a half-connected state. *Assertion: connection status + claimed list + 3 distinct OAuth-failure messages render.* [Source: POC secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md §3.3]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given the Notifications panel, When rendered, Then the event×channel matrix shows only real canon-derived events (no invented events), each cell marked LIVE (real, wired) or COMING (honest badge, real toggle UI, never a fake working switch), autosaving per-toggle with inline 'Saved' + aria-live. *Assertion: LIVE/COMING correctly distinguished; autosave per-toggle.* [Source: POC secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md §3.5]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given the Danger zone, When Delete account is initiated, Then it requires a fresh-session re-auth gate before exposing the confirm, then typed-confirmation (email or 'DELETE'), and renders the ratified consequence copy stating published-and-used skills are unclaimed (never deleted) while drafts/Library/Memory are erased. *Assertion: re-auth gate + typed-confirm + correct unclaim-not-delete copy.* [Source: POC secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md §3.7, SC-2/SC-2b]
- [ ] **AC5** — Given the Appearance panel, When rendered, Then it offers a Light/Dark/System radiogroup, each option applying `<html data-theme>` instantly (persisted to localStorage, set before first paint, no flash), matching orvex-ds.css exactly in both themes. *Assertion: theme selection persists + applies without flash.* [Source: POC secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md §3.6]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: TestAccountSettingsPanelsAutosaveAndDangerGates (AC1/AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: sectioned settings shell + 3 net-new deep-linkable panels (AC1); Connections GitHub status + claimed list + reputation chip + Wiki card (AC2); Notifications event×channel matrix with honest LIVE/COMING (AC3); Danger-zone re-auth-gated typed-confirm delete (AC4); Appearance theme radiogroup (AC5).

## 🧠 Context

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep — orvex-prompt-studio-poc/design-artifacts/C-UX-Scenarios (UX scenario specs, source-of-truth for the studio rewrite). E2-S1 (Signed-in shell) already builds the account-FOOTER MENU (the entry point); this story builds the settings PAGES it links to. E3-S4 already covers the Privacy panel's memory-specific sensitive-field behavior — this story's Connections/Notifications/Danger/Appearance panels are the remaining 4 of the 6-panel IA and must reuse (not re-spec) E3-S4's Privacy panel and Clerk's Profile panel. React-front settings surface (CS §6). Seam: BFF account/notification-prefs/GitHub-status endpoints + Clerk delete-account + re-auth. Sibling dependency: E2-S1 (entry menu), E3-S4 (Privacy panel reuse), E7-S3 (billing panels, if colocated).

## 🧪 Testing

`TestAccountSettingsPanelsAutosaveAndDangerGates` (integration) + unit tests on the LIVE/COMING matrix and the re-auth gate. CS §5 mocking: BFF account/notification-prefs fixtures; never mock own panel rendering.

## 📏 Guidance

- CS `6aMAzsYeQb`: §6 shallow view (no client-side domain logic — BFF/api own it) · §11 honesty (no black-box scores, no fabricated proof, honest empty states) · §3 naming.
- SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: honesty lens (no fake data/social proof); reuse-don't-redesign lens (cite the reused surfaces, don't re-spec them).

## 🔗 References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (candidate FR — not yet assigned) · POC `secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md` §3 (walk/consistency-validated synthesis, PO ratifications 2026-06-17 incl. S2 reputation-chip-on-Connections and the SC-2b delete-copy ruling).

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] **Blocked by:** ENG-2109 (Definition Pack).
- [ ] **Related:** E2-S1 (account footer menu, entry point), E3-S4 (Privacy panel, reused not re-built).

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.feature: Save to Wiki capture flow: the 'Save to Wiki' affordance on the Skill Use surface, the Save Sheet overlay (deterministic content mapping from the woven render into a Docmost page), the per-user private Docmost space provisioning states, and the write path (BFF service-account POST /api/pages/create) — the OUT-loop write, distinct from the read-only /app/wiki launchpad
gaps.why_uncovered: E7-S1 ('Your Wiki: omnibox (search+Ask), native read pane, hover cards, wiki activity', FR-UI24) is explicitly scoped as READ-ONLY — its own Context line states 'React-front wiki-consumption surface (CS §6 — read-only; no in-app wiki editing per non-goal)'. The 'Save to Wiki' capture flow is the WRITE side (a Skill-Use-surface button → Save Sheet overlay → POST to Docmost), the opposite direction from Ask/search/read-pane, and appears nowhere in E7-S1's 5 ACs or any other ui story. secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md's per-route action inventory independently corroborates 'Save to Wiki' as a real, distinct, subordinate action on the Skill Use route (§5.3.4), separate from the read-only launchpad at /app/wiki (also unticketed as a distinct launchpad screen — the E7-S1 read pane is a different, larger surface than the POC's simple explainer+recent-saves+link-out launchpad).
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED — E7-S1 is explicitly scoped read-only ('no in-app wiki editing per non-goal'); this is the write/capture direction. your-wiki-scenario-and-spec.md §2/§5 confirmed present.

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Save to Wiki — capture flow (Save Sheet, deterministic content mapping, Docmost write path)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **signed-in user who just got a useful woven skill render (Lena/Priya)**, I want **a subordinate 'Save to Wiki' action on the Skill Use surface that opens a Save Sheet and files the answer into my own Docmost space**, so that **useful AI answers are captured into a home I own, distinct from Copy and distinct from Memory capture-back** (your-wiki-scenario-and-spec.md §2).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestSaveToWikiWritesDeterministicPageToDocmost` — an integration test asserting: (a) 'Save to Wiki' renders as a subordinate `.btn-soft` action on Skill Use that never outranks the Tier-1 Copy button, (b) tapping it opens the Save Sheet overlay (focus-trapped, shared overlay contract) with the deterministic content mapping pre-filled from the woven render, (c) confirming issues a real POST to the BFF which writes to the user's provisioned private Docmost space via the service-account write path, and (d) every fail-soft state (space not yet provisioned, Docmost unreachable, offline) renders an honest inline message — never a silent failure or a fabricated 'Saved' confirmation. *Final H1–H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given the Skill Use surface (`/app/s/:skillId/use`), When rendered for a signed-in user, Then 'Save to Wiki' appears as a subordinate `.btn-soft` action that never outranks the Tier-1 Copy button, and is absent for anonymous users (Wiki is signed-in only). *Assertion: subordinate placement; anon-absent.* [Source: POC your-wiki-scenario-and-spec.md §6.5, secondary-screens-and-shell-spec.md §5.3.4]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given 'Save to Wiki' is tapped, When the Save Sheet opens, Then it is a focus-trapped overlay (shared overlay contract) pre-filled via the deterministic content-mapping rules (the woven render's typed blocks mapped onto Docmost page content, never an AI-generated summary). *Assertion: deterministic pre-fill, no LLM in the mapping.* [Source: POC your-wiki-scenario-and-spec.md §2.2, §2.3]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given the Save Sheet is confirmed, When the write executes, Then the BFF issues a real POST to `/api/pages/create` against the user's provisioned PRIVATE Docmost space via the service-account auth path (never the user's own Clerk-SSO session for the write), and a real confirmation renders only after the write succeeds (never an optimistic fake-render of Docmost content). *Assertion: real write; confirmation gated on success.* [Source: POC your-wiki-scenario-and-spec.md §2.4, §5.3, §5.4]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given the user's private Docmost space has not yet been provisioned, When Save to Wiki is used for the first time, Then provisioning runs as a real prerequisite step with an honest in-flight state (never a silent stall or a fabricated success). *Assertion: first-save provisioning state renders honestly.* [Source: POC your-wiki-scenario-and-spec.md §1.3 Journey C, §2.6]
- [ ] **AC5** — Given Docmost is unreachable or the user is offline, When Save to Wiki is attempted, Then it fails-soft inline with a clear retry (never queued as silently-done, never blocking Copy or any other action on the page). *Assertion: fail-soft, never silent, never blocks Copy.* [Source: POC your-wiki-scenario-and-spec.md §5.7, §2.5]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: TestSaveToWikiWritesDeterministicPageToDocmost (AC2/AC3/AC5).
- [ ] GREEN: subordinate Save-to-Wiki action on Skill Use (AC1); Save Sheet overlay + deterministic content mapping (AC2); BFF write path + success-gated confirmation (AC3); first-save provisioning state (AC4); fail-soft states (AC5).

## 🧠 Context

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep — orvex-prompt-studio-poc/design-artifacts/C-UX-Scenarios (UX scenario specs, source-of-truth for the studio rewrite). Distinct from E7-S1, which is explicitly read-only ('no in-app wiki editing per non-goal') — this is the write/capture direction. Also distinct from Memory's capture-back (M4.3, E5-S2) which saves a FACT into Memory (goes IN); this saves an ARTIFACT into the Wiki (comes OUT) — the two must never blur per the POC's own D-2 sibling-not-same-loop note. React-front capture surface (CS §6 — the content-mapping is deterministic/presentational, no LLM). Seam: BFF wiki-write + Docmost provisioning. Sibling dependency: E4-S2/E4-S3 (hosts the Skill Use render this action mounts on), E1-S6 (entitlement gating if any).

## 🧪 Testing

`TestSaveToWikiWritesDeterministicPageToDocmost` (integration) + unit tests on the deterministic content-mapping rules. CS §5 mocking: BFF wiki-write/provisioning fixtures; never mock own Save-Sheet rendering.

## 📏 Guidance

- CS `6aMAzsYeQb`: §6 shallow view (no client-side domain logic — BFF/api own it) · §11 honesty (no black-box scores, no fabricated proof, honest empty states) · §3 naming.
- SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: honesty lens (no fake data/social proof); reuse-don't-redesign lens (cite the reused surfaces, don't re-spec them).

## 🔗 References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (candidate FR — not yet assigned; distinct from FR-UI24's read-only Ask surface) · POC `your-wiki-scenario-and-spec.md` §2, §5 (PO-decided 2026-06-17 D-5 in-app /app/wiki route + D-2 sibling-not-same-loop).

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] **Blocked by:** ENG-2109 (Definition Pack).
- [ ] **Related:** E7-S1 (read-only Wiki surface, distinct direction), E5-S2 (Memory capture-back, the sibling IN-loop that must not blur with this OUT-loop).

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.
